title: Used VW Passat (B8) Buying Guide: Risks & What to Check | AutoVerdict
description: Buying a used VW Passat B8? What to check first — DSG service, 2.0 TDI emissions system, adaptive suspension, and key questions for the seller.
intro: The B8 Passat is a common high-mileage family and fleet car, which means servicing history matters more than usual. Here is what to verify before you go and view one.

faq.q: Is the VW Passat B8 reliable?
faq.a: The B8 Passat is generally a dependable long-distance car, but many were used as company or fleet vehicles and cover high mileage. A complete service record and evidence of DSG and emissions maintenance matter more than the odometer figure alone.

faq.q: What should I check on a used Passat 2.0 TDI?
faq.a: On the common 2.0 TDI, check the DPF and EGR have given no trouble, that AdBlue (on later cars) works correctly, and that the DSG gearbox shifts smoothly. Ask whether the car did regular motorway runs rather than only short trips.

faq.q: Is a high-mileage Passat worth buying?
faq.a: A well-serviced high-mileage Passat can be a sound buy, since these engines are designed for distance. The risk is a car that skipped services or was driven only on short journeys. Documentation is what tells the two apart.
